CVEDetector: {"Source": "CVE FEED","Title": "CVE-2026-2892 - Otter Blocks <= 3.1.4 - improper authorization to unauthenticated purchase verification bypass via forged cookie","Content": "CVE ID :CVE-2026-2892Published : April 30, 2026, 2:16 p.m. | 58 minutes agoDescription :The Otter Blocks plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Purchase Verification Bypass in all versions up to, and including, 3.1.4. This is due to the 'get_customer_data' method relying on an unsigned 'o_stripe_data' cookie to determine Stripe product ownership for unauthenticated users. The 'check_purchase' method trusts this cookie data without performing server-side verification against the Stripe API for one-time 'payment' mode purchases. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to bypass Stripe purchase-gated content visibility conditions by forging the 'o_stripe_data' cookie with a target product ID, which is publicly exposed in the checkout block's HTML source.Severity: 7.5 | HIGHVisit the link for more details, such as CVSS details, affected products, timeline, and more...","Detection Date": "30 Apr 2026","Type": "Vulnerability"}🔹t.me/cvedetector🔹
